bead.title: cli: --global install/update surface with agent-tier + project-tier skill links
bead.description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx install` and `ddx update` must support two distinct targets: install-to-agent (global, machine-wide) and install-in-project. Operators who want one Claude Code install per machine (the common case) otherwise repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:54 `runInstall` and cli/cmd/install.go:184 `resolveInstallDirs` must select install root + skill dirs from a --global flag; cli/cmd/install.go:202 `selectInstallState` must pick the global vs project state file. Without a --global path, installs only ever target ddxroot.Path().
- cli/cmd/update.go:58 `runUpdate`, cli/cmd/update.go:204 `performUpdate`, and cli/cmd/update.go:300 `performGlobalUpdate` must split: --global updates only ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (via ddxroot.GlobalDir()), while the default path updates the active project install in whichever mode (in-tree vs convention) is live.

PROPOSED FIX
1. `--global` on `ddx install`/`ddx update`: resolve target ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/, write content there, and create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In in-tree mode those point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/; in convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) they point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/.
3. `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active; `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Runtime plugin lookup precedence (sibling bead: registry three-layer lookup) — this bead consumes that resolver, does not implement it.
- `ddx init` umbrella/lazy behavior (sibling bead: init --global).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/docs (sibling bead: doctor + docs).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry three-layer lookup bead (install targets must match the resolver's precedence paths).
bead.acceptance:
1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves the project tree untouched.
5. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
